content.full_text:
June 19, 1975

George H. Murphy  
Legislative Counsel  
of California  
3021 State Capitol  
Sacramento, Calif. 95814

Re: Request for Opinion  
No. 75-130

Dear Mr. Murphy:

Thank you for your letter of June 3, 1975, requesting an opinion with respect to the duties of state Senators under the Political Reform Act.

The rules of the Commission require that the identity of the person requesting an opinion be disclosed. Since the identity of the state Senator in question has not been disclosed, no formal opinion may be issued.

For your information, however, Section 84305 is applicable to mailings which support or oppose a state candidate. The question as to whether a particular mailing supports or opposes a state candidate is one of fact. The fact that the candidate in question has or has not filed a declaration of candidacy has no relevance.

Although for the reason stated no formal opinion will be issued, I hope the foregoing is sufficiently helpful. If you have any further questions, I urge you to contact a member of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

[signature]  
Daniel H. Lowenstein  
Chairman, for the Commission
